title: Fresh Tender Irish Chicken Stew
description: A low‑calorie, skinless chicken stew with fresh vegetables, finished with toasted rye bread for a festive Irish twist.
instructions: Heat the olive oil in a large Dutch oven over medium heat.
Add the diced onion and sauté until translucent, about 3 minutes.
Stir in the minced garlic and cook for 30 seconds until fragrant.
Add the chicken cubes and brown lightly on all sides, about 4 minutes.
Pour in the chicken broth and add carrots, celery, thyme, rosemary, and bay leaf.
Season with salt and pepper, then bring the mixture to a gentle boil.
Reduce heat to low, cover, and simmer for 25 minutes, or until the chicken is cooked through and the vegetables are tender (internal temp 165°F).
Remove the bay leaf and check seasoning, adding more salt or pepper if needed.
Serve the stew hot, accompanied by toasted rye bread slices on the side.
Enjoy a warm, festive Irish dinner that stays low in calories but high in flavor.
